question_structure.context: In the first quadrant, there is a uniform magnetic field perpendicular to the plane of the paper and pointing outward. In the second quadrant, there is a uniform electric field directed horizontally to the right. In this plane, a particle with mass $m$ and charge $+q$ enters the uniform electric field from point P on the x-axis with an initial velocity $v_0$ perpendicular to the x-axis. The particle exits the electric field at point Q, making a $45^\circ$ angle with the y-axis. After some time, the particle exits perpendicular to the x-axis. The distance between O and P is given as $d$. (Neglect the gravitational force on the particle.)
question_structure.sub_question_1: Find the coordinates of point Q on the y-axis.
question_structure.sub_question_2: Find the magnitude of the magnetic induction of a uniform magnetic field.

sub_question_1.step_1: The particle undergoes projectile-like motion in an electric field. Perpendicular to the direction of the electric field, the particle undergoes uniform linear motion: $y = v_{0} t$
sub_question_1.step_2: Along the direction of the electric field, the particle undergoes uniformly accelerated linear motion with an initial velocity of zero: $x = d = \frac{1}{2} a t^{2}$
sub_question_1.step_4: By solving step_2, step_3, and step_4 simultaneously, we obtain: $y = 2d$
sub_question_1.step_5: The coordinates of point Q on the y-axis are (0, 2d).

sub_question_2.step_6: The particle leaves the electric field and is subjected to the Lorentz force in a uniform magnetic field, performing uniform circular motion. From the geometric relationship, it can be known that the radius of the trajectory circle satisfies: $R = \frac{y}{sin \alpha}$
sub_question_2.step_7: The centripetal force is provided by the Lorentz force: $q v B = m \frac{v^{2}}{R}$
sub_question_2.step_8: The velocity of the particle entering the magnetic field: $v = \frac{v_{0}}{cos \alpha}$
sub_question_2.step_9: Combing step_6, step_7 and step_8, the solution is: $B = \frac{m v_{0}}{2 q d}$

image_captions: The image depicts a two-dimensional Cartesian coordinate system with the x-axis horizontal and y-axis vertical. There is a uniform, horizontal magnetic field directed to the right, represented by horizontal arrows. In the upper right quadrant (x>0, y>0) there is a grid of uniformly spaced dots. At the origin, there is a label O.  A point labeled P lies to the left of the origin on the x-axis, from which a vertical arrow labeled v0 points upward.

result_quantity.name: Vertical displacement of the particle in the electric field
Horizontal displacement of the particle in the electric field

step_3.physical_theorem: Tangent of angle in projectile motion

result_quantity.name: Ratio of vertical velocity to horizontal velocity at point Q
Radius of circular motion of the particle in magnetic field
Relationship between Lorentz force and centripetal force
Velocity of particle entering magnetic field
